claim: A recent National Audit Office (NAO) report says pupil numbers have fallen by 3% since 2018-19, and are projected to fall a further 7% in the next five years
article_title: How pupils with special educational needs are more likely to see their schools close

claim: In the schools that closed between 2020 and 2025, nearly 30% of children had special educational needs, compared with a rate of approximately 20% in the wider school population
article_title: How pupils with special educational needs are more likely to see their schools close

claim: Their research, published on Tuesday, found that 34% of people in 2025 agreed a university education "just isn't worth the amount of time and money" - up from 14% in 2005
article_title: Student loan inquiry begins as a third of people say university degree not worth it

claim: At the same time, there has been a decline in those who believe going to university leaves graduates "a lot better off" in the long run, down from 50% in 2005 to 36% in 2025
article_title: Student loan inquiry begins as a third of people say university degree not worth it

claim: Graduates in England repay 9% of whatever they earn over a threshold, which is due to be frozen at £29,385 from April 2027 for three years - with the result that more graduates will start repaying earlier
article_title: Student loan inquiry begins as a third of people say university degree not worth it
